moments.name: Safety briefing and PPE check
moments.description: Voice-over introduction covering mandatory PPE requirements for EAF steel tapping operations.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Welcome to EAF Steel Tapping Safety Training. Before we begin any tapping operations, it is critical that you understand and verify all Personal Protective Equipment requirements. This training will ensure you are properly equipped to safely perform steel tapping procedures in the Electric Arc Furnace environment.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Mandatory PPE includes: heat-resistant safety hat, fire retardant uniform, aluminized gloves, safety shoes, dark cobalt safety glasses, dust mask N95, and face shield. All equipment must be inspected and worn correctly at all times during tapping operations.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember: Never proceed with any tapping operation without complete PPE. Your safety and the safety of your team depend on strict adherence to these requirements. Let's begin the pre-tap inspection process.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"PPE briefing complete. You are now ready to proceed with the pre-tap inspection procedures.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF facility environment with ambient machinery hum, player receiving safety briefing before beginning tapping operations.

moments.name: Inspect ladle condition and temperature
moments.description: Player inspects the ladle and verifies temperature is within acceptable range.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now approach the ladle and inspect its condition. Touch the ladle to check the temperature reading. It must be between 1000 and 1200 degrees Celsius before tapping can proceed.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Temperature reading confirmed at 1150 degrees Celsius. The ladle is within the acceptable temperature range and ready for tapping operations.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember to touch the ladle to inspect its temperature. Move closer and reach out to the highlighted ladle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You must inspect the ladle temperature before proceeding. Touch the ladle now to complete this critical safety check.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. Ladle temperature verified and within safe operating parameters. You may now proceed to the next step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel mill interior with large metal ladle positioned near furnace base, worker reaching to touch ladle surface to check temperature readout, ambient orange glow from furnace heat

moments.name: Verify ferroalloys ready in bunkers
moments.description: Player inspects ferroalloy hopper to confirm material availability before tapping operation

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before tapping, we need to verify that our ferroalloy bunkers are loaded and ready. Touch the ferroalloy hopper to inspect material availability. This ensures we have the necessary additives for the refining process.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Material levels confirmed. BIN SHP01 and SHP02 are both loaded with ferroalloys. We're ready to proceed with the tapping operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the ferroalloy hopper elevated above ground level. Touch it to verify the material is loaded and ready.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The ferroalloy hopper is directly in front of you. Reach out and touch it to complete the inspection. This is a critical safety check before tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Pre-tap inspection complete. All critical systems verified and ready. Now we can move to the tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel mill environment showing elevated ferroalloy hopper system with bunkers positioned 3 meters above ground level, player reaching to inspect material loading status

moments.name: Turn on local control switch
moments.description: Activate the local control switch to power up the console system.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now that you're at the control pulpit, locate the local control switch on the center console at chest height. Press this button to the ON position to power up the system.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. The local control switch is now ON. Notice the green LED indicator is illuminated, confirming the console has powered up successfully.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the local control switch on the center console. It should be at chest height directly in front of you.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Reach out and press the local control switch button to turn it ON. You need to activate this before proceeding with the tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Great work. The local control system is now active and ready for the next steps in the tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF control pulpit with operator reaching toward illuminated local control switch on center console panel

moments.name: Move ladle transfer car to tapping position
moments.description: Press the EAF Preheat/Charge Position button to position the ladle car beneath the furnace tapping point.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now that the ladle is in position, we need to move the ladle transfer car beneath the furnace tapping point. Locate the EAF Preheat Charge Position button on the main console.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Press the highlighted button to move the ladle car into the tapping position. Watch as the car moves along the tracks to align beneath the furnace.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good! The ladle car is now moving into position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect. The ladle car is now positioned directly beneath the furnace tapping point and ready to receive molten steel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the EAF Preheat Charge Position button on the console. It should be highlighted to help you locate it.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The EAF Preheat Charge Position button is on the main control console. Press it to move the ladle car beneath the furnace tapping point.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! The ladle transfer car is now correctly positioned for the tapping operation. Let's continue with the next step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel mill control console with EAF Preheat Charge Position button highlighted, ladle transfer car visible on tracks moving beneath large electric arc furnace structure

moments.name: Position material addition chute to working position
moments.description: Player rotates selector switch CCW to position chute for tapping operation

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now, position the material addition chute to its working position. Locate the RCH01 selector switch on the left panel of the console and rotate it counterclockwise. This will move the chute into position for the tapping operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The material addition chute is now in the working position. The indicator light confirms the chute is properly positioned for the tapping operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to rotate the RCH01 selector switch counterclockwise to position the material addition chute. Look for the highlighted switch on the left panel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The RCH01 selector switch is right in front of you on the left panel. Grab it with your controller and rotate it counterclockwise to move the chute into working position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The material addition chute is now correctly positioned. Let's continue with the tapping preparation sequence.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial control console with RCH01 selector switch highlighted, overhead material addition chute rotating into working position, operator at console in EAF facility

moments.name: Verify bath temperature and composition
moments.description: Check sampling console display to confirm bath temperature reaches 1630°C target before proceeding with tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before tapping, we must verify the bath temperature and composition. Approach the sampling console and check that the display shows our target temperature of 1630 degrees Celsius, plus or minus 10 degrees. This ensures the steel is at optimal temperature for safe tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect. The display confirms 1630 degrees Celsius. Temperature is within acceptable range. The bath composition meets our specifications, and we are ready to proceed with the tapping operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"You need to check the sampling console display first. Look for the temperature reading on the screen.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The sampling console is highlighted in front of you. Touch the display to verify the bath temperature reading.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Reach out and touch the sampling console display directly in front of you. We must confirm the 1630 degree Celsius target temperature before proceeding.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Temperature verified. With all pre-tapping checks complete, we can now proceed to the actual tapping sequence.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant pulpit control area with trainee reaching toward illuminated digital sampling console display showing temperature readout of 1630°C, with electric arc furnace glowing orange in background

moments.name: Enable tilting control
moments.description: Activate the furnace tilting system by rotating the key switch and pressing the enable button.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now you need to enable the tilting control system. First, rotate the furnace tilting key switch to the ON position. You'll find it on the upper section of the console. After that, press the adjacent Tilt Enable button to activate the hydraulic system.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good! The key switch is now in the ON position. Now press the Tilt Enable button to activate the hydraulic unlock system.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to complete both steps in order. First rotate the key switch to ON, then press the Tilt Enable button.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look at the console. The furnace tilting key switch is on the upper section. Rotate it to ON first, then press the adjacent Tilt Enable button to activate the hydraulic system.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! The tilting control system is now enabled. You should hear the hydraulic unlock sound, and the indicator is illuminated. The furnace is ready for tilting operations.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF control console with illuminated furnace tilting key switch in ON position and pressed Tilt Enable button, hydraulic system activated with indicator lights glowing

moments.name: Start ladle stirring
moments.description: Select stirring gas and set flow rate to begin ladle stirring process.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to start the ladle stirring process. Select either Argon or Nitrogen from the gas selection buttons on the right panel, then set the gas flow to a maximum of 300 liters per minute. This ensures proper mixing of the molten steel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! Argon gas flow has been activated. The gas flow is set to 300 liters per minute. You can hear the stirring process beginning as the gas bubbles through the molten steel, ensuring proper mixing and temperature uniformity.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! Nitrogen gas flow has been activated. The gas flow is set to 300 liters per minute. You can hear the stirring process beginning as the gas bubbles through the molten steel, ensuring proper mixing and temperature uniformity.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to select either the Argon or Nitrogen button on the right panel to start the stirring process. Look for the highlighted buttons.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Touch either the Argon Selection button or the Nitrogen Selection button to activate gas flow at 300 liters per minute. These buttons are directly in front of you on the right control panel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect! The ladle stirring system is now active with the gas flow properly set. This ensures the molten steel will be thoroughly mixed during the tapping process, maintaining consistent quality throughout the ladle.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant control panel with gas selection buttons highlighted, flow meters active, trainee reaching toward Argon or Nitrogen selection controls

moments.name: Unlock EBT flap
moments.description: Trainee simultaneously presses ExtrastrokeBYpass and Unlock_01 buttons to unlock the EBT flap mechanism.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Welcome to the EBT opening procedure. You are now standing at the lower console panel. To unlock the EBT flap, you must press both the Extra Stroke Bypass button and the Unlock button simultaneously. These buttons are located on the panel in front of you, spaced about 15 centimeters apart. Press both buttons at the same time to proceed.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! You have successfully unlocked the EBT flap. The unlock indicator has changed state, and the mechanism is now ready for the next step. You can now proceed to open the EBT flap.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to press both buttons simultaneously. Try placing one hand on each button and pressing them at the same time.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You need to press both the Extra Stroke Bypass button and the Unlock button at the same time. Look for the green outlines highlighting both buttons on the console panel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"To unlock the EBT flap, you must press the Extra Stroke Bypass button with one hand and the Unlock button with your other hand simultaneously. Both buttons are now highlighted. Press them together now.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel furnace control panel with two illuminated buttons, trainee reaching with both hands to press ExtrastrokeBYpass and Unlock buttons simultaneously in dimly lit control room

moments.name: Open EBT flap
moments.description: Trainee presses ExtrastrokeBYpass and Opening buttons simultaneously to open the EBT flap.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now you need to open the EBT flap. Locate the Extra Stroke Bypass button and the Opening button on the lower control panel. Press both buttons simultaneously to activate the hydraulic system and open the flap.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! You have successfully opened the EBT flap. The hydraulic system is now active and the sand is flowing. The flap is fully open and ready for the tapping operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Remember, you must press both the Extra Stroke Bypass button and the Opening button at the same time to open the EBT flap. Try again.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You need to press both buttons simultaneously. Look for the Extra Stroke Bypass button and the Opening button on the lower control panel. Press them both at the same time.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Press the Extra Stroke Bypass button with one hand and the Opening button with your other hand at exactly the same time. Both buttons are highlighted on the lower control panel.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel furnace control panel with Extra Stroke Bypass and Opening buttons highlighted, hydraulic flap beginning to open as sand drops

moments.name: Observe EBT sand drop and steel flow
moments.description: Monitor the furnace and ladle for steel flow initiation over a minimum 3-minute period.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now observe the EBT sand drop and steel flow carefully. You must monitor the furnace and ladle for at least three minutes to ensure proper flow initiation. Watch for the molten steel stream to become visible below the EBT opening.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. You have successfully monitored the steel flow for the required three minutes. The molten steel stream is now visible and flowing properly from the furnace into the ladle. The tapping process is proceeding as expected.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember to carefully observe both the furnace and the ladle. Look for the molten steel stream becoming visible below the EBT opening. Continue monitoring until the timer reaches three minutes.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Focus your attention on the furnace base and the ladle. The steel flow must be monitored for the full three-minute duration to ensure safe and proper tapping. Watch the area below the EBT opening where the molten steel stream should be visible.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Steel flow monitoring complete. You've successfully observed the tapping process for the required duration. Let's proceed to the next step in the EBT opening procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant furnace tapping area with operator viewing molten steel flowing from EBT opening into large ladle, bright orange steel stream visible, timer display showing 3 minutes elapsed

moments.name: Use Oxygen lance if steel does not flow
moments.description: Trainee grabs and uses oxygen lancing pipe to clear EBT blockage and restore steel flow.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"If steel does not flow after opening the EBT, we must clear the blockage using an oxygen lance. The lancing pipe is stored on the wall rack to your right. Grab the lance, aim it carefully toward the tap hole, and insert it to ignite and clear the obstruction. Be extremely cautious—the aluminum jacket can melt and cause splashing.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Now carefully aim the lance toward the tap hole and insert it into the EBT opening. Watch for ignition and the resumption of steel flow.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! The blockage is cleared and steel flow has resumed. Always exercise extreme caution when using the oxygen lance—molten aluminum can cause dangerous splashing. You have successfully opened the EBT.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, the lancing pipe is on the wall rack to your right. Grab it to clear the blockage in the tap hole.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You must grab the oxygen lancing pipe from the wall rack and use it to clear the EBT blockage. Reach out with your hand and grab it now.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Outstanding work. The EBT is now fully open and steel is flowing into the ladle. We will now proceed to the next phase of the tapping operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel furnace facility with trainee grabbing oxygen lancing pipe from wall rack and aiming toward EBT tap hole as molten steel flows into ladle below

moments.name: Wait until 20-25 tons in ladle
moments.description: Monitor the ladle weight display until it reaches 20-25 tons threshold.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Monitor the ladle weight display carefully. We need to wait until the ladle reaches between 20 and 25 tons before we can begin adding ferroalloys. Watch the weight indicator on your console.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect. The ladle has reached the target weight of 20 to 25 tons. We're now ready to begin adding ferroalloys to adjust the steel composition.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good work monitoring the ladle weight. Let's proceed to the next step of adding ferroalloys.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant console view showing ladle weight display reaching 20-25 ton threshold, with the EAF ladle filling below in the background.

moments.name: Open ferroalloy slide gates
moments.description: Trainee simultaneously presses both slide gate open buttons to discharge ferroalloys into the ladle during tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to discharge ferroalloys into the ladle. Locate the slide gate open buttons on the upper right panel. You'll need to press both BinShip01 and BinShip02 slide gate open buttons simultaneously to allow ferroalloys to discharge through the chute overhead.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to press both slide gate open buttons simultaneously. Look for the green highlighted buttons on the upper right panel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Press both BinShip01 and BinShip02 slide gate open buttons at the same time. They are located on the upper right control panel. This will allow the ferroalloys to discharge into the ladle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! Both slide gates are now open and ferroalloys are discharging through the chute into the ladle. The gate indicators show open position. This ensures proper alloy addition during the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant control panel with two green-highlighted slide gate open buttons on upper right section, ferroalloy discharge chute visible overhead, ladle positioned below ready to receive material flow.

moments.name: Move chute to parking position before 2 degree tilt
moments.description: Trainee presses the RCH01 park position selector switch to CW before the furnace tilts 2 degrees.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"As the furnace prepares to tilt further, you must move the RCH01 chute to its parking position. Locate the RCH01InParkPosition selector switch on the left panel and press it to CW before the furnace reaches 2 degrees tilt. This ensures the chute clears the tapping path safely.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. The RCH01 chute is now safely parked and clear of the tapping path. The indicator confirms CW position. You've completed the ferroalloy addition sequence correctly.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"That's not the correct control. Focus on the RCH01InParkPosition selector switch on the left panel. You must move the chute to park position before the furnace tilts 2 degrees.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, the RCH01 chute must be moved to its parking position before the furnace tilts 2 degrees. Look for the RCH01InParkPosition selector switch on the left panel and press it to CW.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Time is critical. Press the RCH01InParkPosition selector switch to CW now. It's the green switch on the left panel. Failure to park the chute before 2 degree tilt creates a collision hazard.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Well done. You've successfully completed the ferroalloy addition sequence and parked the chute safely. The furnace can now continue its tilt for tapping. Let's proceed to the next phase.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel mill EAF control station showing RCH01InParkPosition selector switch on left control panel with furnace tilting mechanism visible in background, tilt gauge reading below 2 degrees, chute retracting to safe park position with green indicator light confirming clearance.

moments.name: Open circuit breaker and lift electrodes
moments.description: Rotate the furnace breaker key switch to open the circuit and observe the electrodes automatically lift to their tapping position.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before we can tap the furnace, we need to open the circuit breaker and lift the electrodes. Locate the furnace breaker key switch on the upper section of the console and rotate it to open the breaker. This will automatically lift the electrodes to their tapping position and extinguish the arc.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good! The circuit breaker is now open. Notice how the electrodes are lifting automatically to their tapping position and the electric arc has been extinguished. This is a critical safety step before tapping begins.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the furnace breaker key switch on the upper section of the console. You need to rotate it to open the circuit breaker.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Grab the furnace breaker key switch with your controller and rotate it. This will open the breaker and trigger the electrodes to lift automatically.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! The circuit breaker is open and the electrodes are in tapping position. We're now ready to proceed with the next step of the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial furnace control console with breaker key switch prominently displayed, large electrodes visible through observation window beginning to rise upward from molten metal bath

moments.name: Tilt furnace gradually
moments.description: Trainee pushes the left joystick forward to tilt the furnace smoothly while avoiding contact with water-cooled panels.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now, push the left joystick forward to gradually tilt the furnace. Control the tilt rate carefully to ensure a smooth steel pour. Watch the tilt angle and be cautious not to let the furnace contact the water-cooled panels.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good, you've grabbed the left joystick. Now push it forward smoothly to start tilting the furnace.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! The furnace is tilting. Monitor the tilt angle closely. Steel is now pouring into the ladle. Maintain steady control to avoid any contact with the water-cooled panels.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"That's not correct. Use the left joystick and push it forward to tilt the furnace. Make sure you control the tilt rate smoothly.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, grab the left joystick and push it forward to start the furnace tilt. Control is key to avoid water-cooled panel contact.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Final reminder: Grab the left joystick with your right hand and push it forward to tilt the furnace. This controls the steel pouring process.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Well done! You've successfully tilted the furnace and initiated the steel pouring process. The tilt control is critical for safe and efficient tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel furnace floor with Electric Arc Furnace tilting forward as molten steel begins pouring into a large ladle below, operator holding left joystick on remote controller, ambient orange glow from molten metal, heavy machinery visible in background.

moments.name: Tilt back at 5-7 tons remaining
moments.description: Pull joystick backward to de-slag when 5-7 tons remain in furnace

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You're nearing the end of tapping. When the weight display shows between 5 and 7 tons remaining—approximately 150 to 155 tons total—pull the left joystick backward to tilt the furnace back for de-slagging. This separates remaining slag from the steel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good, you've grabbed the joystick. Now pull it backward to initiate the de-slagging tilt.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. The furnace is now tilting backward to the de-slagging position. The remaining slag will separate from the steel, allowing for proper finishing.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, grab the left joystick and pull it backward to tilt the furnace back for de-slagging.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You must tilt the furnace back now. Grab the left joystick with your left hand and pull it backward to de-slag the remaining steel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect execution. You've successfully tilted the furnace back for de-slagging with 5 to 7 tons remaining. This ensures proper slag separation and maintains steel quality.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF furnace tilting backward in reverse motion as operator pulls joystick, slag separating from molten steel, weight display showing 150-155 tons

moments.name: Move ladle to preheat/charging position
moments.description: Operator presses position button to move ladle car to preheat zone after tapping completion

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now that tapping is complete, we need to move the ladle car to the preheat and charging position. Locate the position button on the main console and press it to move the ladle car away from the furnace to the preheater zone.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. The ladle car is now moving to the preheat and charging position. The ladle is clear of the furnace tapping area.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the preheat charge position button on the main console. It controls the ladle car movement to the preheater zone.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Press the highlighted button to move the ladle car to the preheat and charging position. This clears the tapping area for the next operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The ladle car is now positioned at the preheat and charging zone. The tapping area is clear and ready for the next heat cycle.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant control console with operator pressing position button while ladle car moves along tracks toward preheat charging area

moments.name: Tilt furnace to 7 degrees and clean EBT bottom
moments.description: Operator tilts the furnace to 7 degrees using the joystick and cycles the EBT bottom flap to clean debris.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to prepare the EBT bottom for maintenance. First, tilt the furnace to 7 degrees using the left joystick to ensure safe access. Then cycle the EBT flap by pressing Closing followed by Opening to remove any remaining debris.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good, the furnace is tilting. Watch the angle gauge and stop when it reaches 7 degrees.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect. The EBT flap is closing. This helps clean any residual material from the bottom.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The EBT flap is now open and the bottom is clean. The furnace is ready for sand filling.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"That's not the correct control. Follow the highlighted objects in the correct sequence: first tilt with the joystick, then press Closing, and finally Opening.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to tilt the furnace to 7 degrees first using the left joystick. The green outline shows which control to use.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Let me guide you step by step. First, grab and push the left joystick forward to tilt the furnace to 7 degrees. Then touch the Closing button, and finally touch the Opening button to complete the EBT cleaning cycle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Task completed successfully. The furnace is tilted to 7 degrees and the EBT bottom has been cleaned through the flap cycle. We're now ready to proceed with sand filling.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel furnace facility with operator using left joystick to tilt large furnace to 7 degrees while monitoring angle gauge, with EBT bottom flap cycling mechanism visible beneath tilted furnace

moments.name: Close EBT flap and lock
moments.description: Close the EBT flap and engage the lock to secure it.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to close and lock the EBT flap. First, press the Closing button to close the flap, then press the Lock button to secure it in place.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good, the flap is closing. Now press the Lock button to secure it.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the green outlined button. Press the Closing button first, then the Lock button.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The Closing button is on the lower control panel. Press it to close the EBT flap, then press the Lock button to secure it.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The EBT flap is now closed and locked securely. The system is ready for the next step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF control panel with Closing and Lock buttons, EBT flap mechanism visible below furnace structure, operator pressing sequential controls to secure flap

moments.name: Open EBT cover
moments.description: Trainee presses the power switch to open the EBT access cover for maintenance.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now press the power switch to open the EBT access cover. This will expose the tap hole for maintenance.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. The EBT cover is now open and the tap hole is accessible for sand filling.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the power switch cover button on the control panel. Press it to open the EBT access cover.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The power switch cover button is highlighted. Touch it with your hand to open the EBT access cover.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good work. With the cover open, we can now proceed to the sand filling operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF furnace facility with trainee standing at control panel pressing power switch button as hydraulic cover slides open revealing tap hole access at furnace base

moments.name: Inspect tap hole via monitoring display
moments.description: Check tap hole condition on monitoring display camera feed

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now let's inspect the tap hole condition. Look at the monitoring display on the right console section. The camera feed will show you the tap hole interior. Check for any blockages or erosion that might affect tapping quality.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. The camera feed shows the tap hole interior clearly. The condition appears normal with no visible blockages or excessive erosion. The tap hole is ready for the tapping operation. This inspection is critical to ensure smooth molten steel flow and prevent potential issues during tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Touch the monitoring display to inspect the tap hole condition via the camera feed.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You must inspect the tap hole before proceeding. Touch the monitoring display on the right console section to view the camera feed.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Tap hole inspection complete. The condition is satisfactory and we can proceed with the sand filling operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant EAF control room with operator inspecting tap hole condition on monitoring display, camera feed showing furnace interior on screen

moments.name: Fill EBT with sand
moments.description: Operator presses slide gate to discharge sand through sandgun, forming protective dome over tap hole.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to fill the EBT with sand to protect the tap hole. Locate the BinShip01 slide gate open button on the upper control panel and press it to discharge sand through the sandgun. Watch for sand flow forming a dome-shaped pile over the tap hole.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good! Sand is now flowing through the sandgun into the EBT. Observe the sand forming a protective dome-shaped pile over the tap hole. This sand layer is critical for preventing molten steel from solidifying in the tap hole during the next heat.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to press the BinShip01 slide gate open button to start the sand discharge. It's highlighted in green on the upper control panel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You must press the BinShip01 slide gate open button now. This is the control that opens the sand bin gate to discharge sand through the sandgun into the EBT tap hole. Locate the green highlighted button and press it.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! The EBT has been properly filled with sand, forming the protective dome over the tap hole. This sand layer will prevent molten steel from solidifying and ensure smooth tapping operations in the next heat cycle. Let's proceed to the final step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF facility upper control panel area with BinShip01 slide gate button highlighted in green, sandgun positioned below with visible sand discharge forming dome over tap hole, control interface visible in foreground.

moments.name: Close sand hopper and EBT cover
moments.description: Press close buttons to secure sand system and EBT cover after maintenance

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to secure the EBT area. First, press the BinShip01 slide gate close button to seal the sand hopper. Then press the power switch cover button to close the protective cover over the EBT opening. Complete both actions in order to secure the maintenance area.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Sand gate closed successfully. Now press the power switch cover button to secure the EBT protective cover.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. Both the sand hopper gate and EBT protective cover are now secured. The EBT maintenance and sand filling procedure is complete.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Remember to close the sand gate first, then the protective cover. Follow the correct sequence to properly secure the EBT area.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Press the green highlighted BinShip01 slide gate close button first to seal the sand hopper.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look at the highlighted close button on the sand hopper control panel. Press it to close the slide gate, then move to the power switch cover button.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"EBT maintenance and sand filling complete. The furnace is now ready for tapping operations. Moving to final verification.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EBT maintenance area with control panels, worker pressing close buttons on sand hopper and protective cover controls as systems secure

moments.name: Estimate hot heel quantity
moments.description: Voice-over explains hot heel estimation process and its impact on next heat.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"After tapping is complete, we need to estimate the hot heel quantity. Hot heel refers to the molten steel and slag that remains in the furnace after tapping. On average, this is around 30 tons. This estimation is critical because it directly impacts the amount of DRI we'll charge in the next heat. Accurate hot heel estimation helps maintain consistent furnace operations and optimal heat balance.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Now that you understand hot heel estimation, let's move on to the final shutdown procedures.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: EAF industrial facility floor view with furnace in background after tapping operation, residual glow visible inside furnace vessel indicating hot heel material.

moments.name: Return furnace to horizontal position
moments.description: Trainee uses joystick to return furnace to horizontal position after tapping operation.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now use the left joystick to return the furnace to its horizontal position. Move the joystick back to center until the angle gauge reads zero degrees.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Now move the joystick to return the furnace to horizontal.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. The furnace is returning to horizontal position. Monitor the angle gauge.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"That's not quite right. Use the left joystick to control the furnace tilt angle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, grab the left joystick and move it to return the furnace to zero degrees horizontal.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Grab the left joystick with your left hand and operate it to level the furnace. Watch the angle indicator reach zero degrees.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect! The furnace is now in horizontal position. The tapping operation is nearly complete.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF facility with large furnace returning to horizontal position as operator uses left joystick at control station, hydraulic systems settling with ambient machinery hum.

moments.name: Clean slag door and check fettling material
moments.description: Final post-tap procedure narration covering slag door cleaning and fettling material verification

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we complete the post-tap procedure. Clean the slag door thoroughly to remove any residual material. Check the fettling material level carefully - it must not exceed 10 to 15 centimeters above the apron. This ensures proper furnace operation for the next heat cycle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. You have successfully completed the EAF steel tapping procedure. Remember, safety and precision are critical at every step. You are now ready to perform this operation in a real production environment.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Training complete. You are now prepared to safely execute the EAF tapping operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF facility post-tapping area with slag door visible and fettling material inspection zone, completion of steel tapping training sequence
